factors.summary: Demand trends for the company's services have been mixed, with some weakness observed in 2016 due to economic conditions and freight diversion to lower-cost carriers, though prior years showed growth in LTL shipments and tons. Overall, demand is considered stable with no significant decline anticipated.

evidence.text: Although our revenue for 2016 increased slightly, we believe demand for our service during 2016 was impacted by general weakness in the domestic economy. We believe that some shippers placed more emphasis on the cost of service in 2016 rather than our high-quality total value proposition, which also resulted in some freight diversion to lower cost carriers.
LTL tonnage increased 7.4% primarily due to an 11.6% increase in LTL shipments.
We do not anticipate a significant decline in business levels or financial performance that would cause us to violate any such covenants in the future.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Mixed demand signals with some weakness and prior growth, but overall stable.

factors.summary: The company demonstrates strong pricing power, successfully raising prices in stable environments and maintaining a disciplined yield management process. However, there are mixed results, with LTL revenue per hundredweight decreasing due to fuel surcharge declines but increasing when excluding fuel surcharges. The company faces potential downward pricing pressures due to competition and may struggle to maintain or increase prices. Despite this, the company has implemented fuel surcharge programs with customers to help offset increased diesel fuel costs, indicating some pricing power.

evidence.text: We nonetheless remained committed to our pricing philosophy and focused on providing superior customer service during 2016.
LTL revenue per hundredweight increased 1.5% to $18.51 in 2016 primarily due to our disciplined yield management process and a generally stable pricing environment.
LTL revenue per hundredweight, excluding fuel surcharges, increased 5.7% in 2015 as compared to 2014.

Numerous competitive factors could impair our ability to maintain our current profitability. These factors include...some of our competitors may reduce their prices to gain business, especially during times of reduced growth rates in the economy, which may limit our ability to maintain or increase prices or maintain revenue;
We have fuel surcharge programs in place with a majority of our customers, which help offset the negative impact of the increased cost of diesel fuel and other petroleum-based products.
evidence.subsection: We may be adversely impacted by fluctuations in the availability and price of diesel fuel.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Strong pricing power with increases in revenue per hundredweight and fuel surcharge programs.

factors.summary: The company is focused on organic growth through expanding its service center network and increasing service offerings, but this strategy comes with risks such as increased expenses and management strain.

evidence.text: Although our revenue for 2016 increased slightly...
Revenue in 2016 increased $19.1 million, or 0.6%, as compared to 2015.
Revenue in 2015 increased $184.5 million, or 6.6% as compared to 2014. LTL tonnage increased 7.4% primarily due to an 11.6% increase in LTL shipments.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Focus on organic growth with historical revenue increases despite risks.

factors.summary: The company's operating margins are under pressure, with compression observed due to increased labor and benefit costs, despite some stability in gross margins. There is a risk that margins could further compress if the company fails to execute its growth strategy effectively.

evidence.text: This led to a 60 basis-point increase in our operating ratio to 83.8% as compared to our Company record 83.2% in 2015. Net income decreased $8.9 million, or 2.9%, and diluted earnings per share decreased 0.3% to $3.56 in 2016 as compared to $3.57 in 2015.
Our operating ratio decreased to 83.2% in 2015 from 84.2% in 2014. Aggregate productive labor costs increased to 27.9% of revenue in 2015 as compared to 25.8% in 2014.

If we fail to overcome those risks, we may not realize additional revenue or profits from our efforts, we may incur additional expenses and, as a result, our financial position and results of operations could be materially and adversely affected.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Operating margins under pressure due to increased labor costs and potential compression.

factors.summary: The company is actively increasing its capacity through investments in real estate, equipment, and technology, as well as expanding its network and service offerings, reflecting confidence in its growth strategy.

evidence.text: We spent $417.9 million on real estate, equipment and technology to increase the capacity of our network to accommodate future growth...
Our growth strategy includes [...] selectively expanding our capacity and broadening the scope of our service offerings. [...] We expect to continue to do so.
evidence.subsection: If we are unable to successfully execute our growth strategy, our business and future results of operations may suffer.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Significant investments in capacity expansion reflecting confidence in growth.

factors.summary: Management demonstrates confidence through sustained investments in real estate, equipment, and technology, as well as operational efficiency improvements and stock repurchase programs, while acknowledging the risks associated with their growth strategy.

evidence.text: We also maintained our long-term commitment to the continuous investment in our business in 2016. We spent $417.9 million on real estate, equipment and technology...

We believe depreciation will continue to increase based on our 2017 capital expenditure plan. While our investments in real estate, equipment and technology can increase our costs in the short-term, we believe these investments are necessary to support our continued growth and strategic initiatives.

We also implemented certain operational initiatives that decreased our reliance on purchased transportation providers and increased our utilization of Company employees and equipment. The additional fuel consumption resulted in an increase in fuel taxes, which continued to improve as we add newer, more fuel-efficient equipment to our operations.

We expect to continue to maintain a high level of capital expenditures in order to support our long-term plan for market share growth. During the second quarter of 2016, we completed our $200.0 million stock repurchase program, previously announced on November 10, 2014. On May 23, 2016, we announced that our Board of Directors had approved the 2016 Repurchase Program, under which we may repurchase up to $250.0 million of shares of our common stock from time to time in open market purchases or through privately negotiated transactions.
We cannot assure that we will overcome the risks associated with our growth strategy.
evidence.subsection: If we are unable to successfully execute our growth strategy, our business and future results of operations may suffer.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Sustained investments and operational improvements indicate confidence despite risks.

factors.summary: The company faces several near-term risks, including lower than expected revenue growth, economic weakness, fluctuating diesel fuel prices, increasing labor costs, competitive pressures, and potential impacts from industry consolidation. Additionally, the company may face challenges related to real estate shortages, seasonal factors, harsh weather conditions, and governance provisions that could discourage changes in control or management. These risks could impact profitability, operational efficiency, and overall business performance.

evidence.text: As a result of these investments and lower than expected revenue growth, certain operating costs as a percent of revenue increased during 2016. This led to a 60 basis-point increase in our operating ratio to 83.8% as compared to our Company record 83.2% in 2015. Net income decreased $8.9 million, or 2.9%, and diluted earnings per share decreased 0.3% to $3.56 in 2016 as compared to $3.57 in 2015.

If we are unable to effectively compete with other LTL carriers, whether on the basis of price, service or otherwise, we may be unable to retain existing customers or attract new customers, either of which could have a material adverse effect on our business, financial condition and results of operations;

•shortages of suitable real estate may limit our growth and could cause congestion in our service center network, which could result in increased operating expenses; [...] •expanding our service offerings may require us to enter into new markets and encounter new competitive challenges.

Harsh winter weather or natural disasters, such as hurricanes, tornadoes and floods, can also adversely impact our performance by reducing demand and reducing our ability to transport freight, which could result in decreased revenue and increased operating expenses.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Multiple near-term risks impacting profitability and operational efficiency.

factors.summary: The company has a clear long-term strategic direction focused on capacity expansion, service improvement, market share growth, and operational efficiency. The strategy includes significant capital expenditures for real estate, equipment, and technology to support future growth and meet customer demands, investments in fuel-efficient equipment, and a plan to expand its service center network and service offerings. The company is committed to maintaining a high level of capital expenditures to support its long-term growth objectives.

evidence.text: We also maintained our long-term commitment to the continuous investment in our business in 2016. We spent $417.9 million on real estate, equipment and technology to increase the capacity of our network to accommodate future growth, while also meeting the increasing service demands of our customers.

Our capital expenditures varied based upon the projected increase in the number and size of our service center facilities to support our plan for long-term growth, our planned tractor and trailer replacement cycle and forecasted tonnage and shipment growth. Expenditures for land and structures can be dependent upon the availability of land in the geographic areas where we are looking to expand. We expect to continue to maintain a high level of capital expenditures in order to support our long-term plan for market share growth.

Our growth strategy includes increasing the volume of freight moving through our existing service center network, selectively expanding our capacity and broadening the scope of our service offerings. [...] We expect to continue to do so.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Clear long-term strategic investments in capacity and technology for growth.

factors.summary: The revenue mix shifted towards higher-margin LTL services, as non-LTL revenue decreased due to strategic changes in service offerings.

evidence.text: Our revenue in 2016 increased $19.1 million, or 0.6% as compared to 2015 due to a $45.9 million increase in LTL revenue, partially offset by a $26.8 million reduction in non-LTL revenue.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The shift towards higher-margin LTL services indicates a positive development in revenue structure.

factors.summary: The company has experienced mixed volume trends, with unit volumes growing in recent years but facing seasonal declines. While LTL shipments have shown consistent growth, particularly from 2014 to 2015, there have been slight declines in LTL tons and seasonal reductions in shipments during winter months, impacting revenue growth.

evidence.text: LTL shipments (in thousands) increased 11.6% and LTL tons (in thousands) increased 7.4%. LTL revenue per hundredweight decreased 0.5%, primarily due to declines in our fuel surcharges.
LTL shipments (in thousands) increased by 19 to 10,148 in 2016 compared to 10,129 in 2015, while LTL tons (in thousands) declined by 7 to 7,931 in 2016 compared to 7,938 in 2015.
Our revenue and operating margins in the first and fourth quarters are typically lower than those during the second and third quarters due to reduced shipments during the winter months.
evidence.subsection: Our results of operations may be affected by seasonal factors, harsh weather conditions and disasters.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Mixed volume trends with growth in shipments but declines in tons and seasonal impacts suggest no clear directional signal.

factors.summary: Input costs, particularly diesel fuel, have been a significant and volatile expense for the company. While diesel fuel costs have decreased in recent years due to lower prices, the company faces risks related to fluctuating fuel costs and the effectiveness of its fuel surcharge program. Additionally, real estate shortages could lead to increased operating expenses due to service center congestion. The company is partially offsetting these challenges through operational changes and pricing strategies, but ongoing risks remain.

evidence.text: The decrease in our diesel fuel costs, excluding fuel taxes, during 2016 was due primarily to a 13.4% decline in our average cost per gallon.
Our diesel fuel costs decreased primarily due to a 33.7% decrease in our average cost per gallon during 2015 as compared to the prior year.

we may be unable to continue to collect fuel surcharges or our fuel surcharge program may become ineffective in mitigating the impact of the fluctuating costs of fuel and other petroleum-based products;
shortages of suitable real estate may limit our growth and could cause congestion in our service center network, which could result in increased operating expenses;
evidence.subsection: If we are unable to successfully execute our growth strategy, our business and future results of operations may suffer.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: While diesel fuel costs have decreased, risks from fuel surcharge effectiveness and real estate shortages pose concerns.

factors.summary: Labor costs are increasing, creating margin pressure. The company experienced significant increases in salaries, wages, and benefits in both 2016 and 2015, driven by higher employee numbers and wage hikes. Additionally, the company faces risks related to potential future increases in labor costs due to hiring new employees, which could result in temporary inefficiencies and higher training expenses.

evidence.text: Salaries, wages and benefits increased $188.5 million, or 13.6% in 2015 due to a $143.6 million increase in salaries and wages and a $44.9 million increase in benefit costs.
Salaries, wages and benefits increased $82.3 million, or 5.2% in 2016 due to a $47.1 million increase in salaries and wages and a $35.2 million increase in benefit costs.
•hiring new employees may increase training costs and may result in temporary inefficiencies until those employees become proficient in their jobs;
evidence.subsection: If we are unable to successfully execute our growth strategy, our business and future results of operations may suffer.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Significant increases in labor costs create margin pressure, indicating a negative trend.

factors.summary: The company has implemented cost reduction programs, particularly in purchased transportation, which have resulted in significant savings. These initiatives include the strategic elimination of certain services, leading to a 36.3% decrease in purchased transportation expenses from 2015 to 2016 and a 10.1% decrease from 2014 to 2015.

evidence.text: Purchased transportation decreased $42.2 million, or 36.3% in 2016 as compared to 2015. The decrease was due primarily to the strategic elimination of certain services in the second quarter of 2015 that reduced our use of third-party providers for the remainder of 2015 and throughout 2016.
Purchased transportation expense decreased $13.0 million, or 10.1%, in 2015 as compared to 2014.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Strategic elimination of services leading to significant savings shows a very positive development in cost management.

factors.summary: Productivity has been improving over the two-year period, with a notable 4.0% increase in platform pounds and platform shipments per hour in 2016 compared to 2015, and a 1.8% improvement in platform shipments per hour in 2015 compared to 2014. These advancements indicate a positive trend in operational efficiency.

evidence.text: Platform pounds and platform shipments per hour improved 4.0% and 3.8%, respectively, in 2016 as compared to 2015.

Our aggregate productive labor costs increased to 27.9% of revenue in 2015 as compared to 25.8% in 2014. Additional freight density contributed to a slight improvement in our P&D and platform shipments per hour, which improved 1.1% and 1.8%, respectively, from 2014.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Improvements in productivity metrics indicate a positive trend in operational efficiency.

factors.summary: The company is sensitive to several key factors that could impact its near-term results, including diesel fuel prices and availability, labor costs, economic conditions, competition, and seasonal weather patterns. Diesel fuel prices significantly affect both operating costs and revenue through fuel surcharges, which are indexed weekly. Additionally, labor costs, particularly employee benefit expenses, are a material sensitivity. The company also faces risks from competitors reducing prices during economic downturns and seasonal trends that lower revenue and operating margins in certain quarters.

evidence.text: Most of our tariffs and contracts provide for a fuel surcharge that is generally indexed to the DOE's published diesel fuel prices that reset each week. Our fuel surcharges are designed to offset fluctuations in the cost of petroleum-based products and are one of the many components included in the overall negotiated price we charge for our services.

Employee benefit costs increased $44.9 million, or 13.2%, primarily due to an increase in the number of full-time employees eligible for benefits, certain enhancements to paid-time-off benefits and an increase in our workers compensation expense.

Numerous competitive factors could impair our ability to maintain our current profitability. These factors include...some of our competitors may reduce their prices to gain business, especially during times of reduced growth rates in the economy;

Our operations are subject to seasonal trends common in our industry. Our revenue and operating margins in the first and fourth quarters are typically lower than those during the second and third quarters due to reduced shipments during the winter months.
evidence.subsection: We may be adversely impacted by fluctuations in the availability and price of diesel fuel.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Multiple risks including fuel prices, labor costs, and seasonal trends pose moderate headwinds.

factors.summary: Catalysts include ongoing investments in fuel-efficient equipment and operational efficiency initiatives, which are expected to improve profitability and market position.

evidence.text: Our fuel consumption benefited from an overall improvement in miles per gallon, which continues to improve as we add newer, more fuel-efficient equipment to our operations.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Investments in fuel-efficient equipment and operational efficiency are expected to improve profitability, indicating a positive outlook.

factors.summary: The company is maintaining a high level of capital expenditures to support long-term growth.

evidence.text: We expect to continue to maintain a high level of capital expenditures in order to support our long-term plan for market share growth. We currently estimate capital expenditures will be approximately $385 million for the year ending December 31, 2017.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: The company is using various financing methods for capex, which doesn't indicate a clear positive or negative trend.

factors.summary: The company is maintaining buybacks through its stock repurchase program but did not declare dividends in 2016 or 2015.

evidence.text: During the years ended December 31, 2016, 2015, and 2014, we repurchased 2,062,841, 1,682,419, and 70,620 shares of our common stock under our repurchase programs for an aggregate of $130.3 million, $114.1 million, and $5.5 million, respectively. As of December 31, 2016, we had $200.0 million remaining authorized under the 2016 Repurchase Program. We did not declare or pay a dividend on our common stock in 2016 or 2015.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company is actively repurchasing shares, showing commitment to shareholder value.

factors.summary: The company is at risk of losing market share due to competitive pressures and customer consolidation.

evidence.text: many customers reduce the number of carriers they use by selecting 'core carriers' as approved transportation service providers and we may not be selected;

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: The company faces a significant risk of losing market share due to competitive pressures.

factors.summary: The competitive environment is intensifying due to factors such as new entrants, aggressive rivals, and industry consolidation.

evidence.text: competition from non-asset-based logistics and freight brokerage companies may adversely affect our customer relationships and ability to maintain sufficient pricing;

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Intensifying competition from new entrants and aggressive rivals poses a threat.

factors.summary: The company's competitive differentiation is under pressure as it needs to invest in technology to remain competitive, and customers may not accept higher prices to cover these costs.

evidence.text: advances in technology require increased investments to remain competitive, and our customers may not be willing to accept higher prices to cover the cost of these investments;

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: The company's differentiation is under pressure due to technological investment needs and potential customer resistance.

factors.summary: Economic conditions pose a risk as reduced growth rates can lead to price competition and limit the company's ability to maintain revenue.

evidence.text: some of our competitors may reduce their prices to gain business, especially during times of reduced growth rates in the economy, which may limit our ability to maintain or increase prices or maintain revenue;

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Economic conditions pose risks through reduced growth and potential price competition.

factors.summary: The company is financing its capital expenditures through existing cash, cash flow from operations, debt, and credit agreement.

evidence.text: We generally finance our capital expenditures and planned growth with existing cash, cash flow from operations, issuance of debt and through available borrowings under our existing senior unsecured credit agreement.
evidence.subsection: We have significant ongoing cash requirements that could limit our growth and affect our profitability if we are unable to obtain sufficient capital.

factors.summary: The company is using debt to finance its operations, indicating potential leverage.

evidence.text: We may require additional capital to finance long-term real estate purchase opportunities and acquisitions, which we may fund through additional debt or through equity offerings.
evidence.subsection: We have significant ongoing cash requirements that could limit our growth and affect our profitability if we are unable to obtain sufficient capital.

factors.summary: The company's free cash flow situation is uncertain and may affect its ability to grow.

evidence.text: If our cash requirements are high or our cash flow from operations is low during particular periods, we may need to seek additional financing, which could be costly or difficult to obtain.
evidence.subsection: We have significant ongoing cash requirements that could limit our growth and affect our profitability if we are unable to obtain sufficient capital.

factors.summary: The company faces significant near-term risks related to its cash requirements and dividend payments. High cash requirements could strain profitability and limit growth, potentially necessitating costly or difficult-to-obtain additional financing. Additionally, future dividend payments are not guaranteed and will depend on factors such as financial condition, liquidity, cash needs, and board considerations.

evidence.text: Our business also has significant ongoing operating cash requirements. If our cash requirements are high or our cash flow from operations is low during particular periods, we may need to seek additional financing, which could be costly or difficult to obtain.
evidence.subsection: We have significant ongoing cash requirements that could limit our growth and affect our profitability if we are unable to obtain sufficient capital.

evidence.text: As a result, future dividend payments are not guaranteed and will depend upon various factors such as our overall financial condition, available liquidity, anticipated cash needs, future prospects for earnings and cash flows, as well as other factors considered relevant by our Board of Directors.
evidence.subsection: There can be no assurance of our ability to declare and pay cash dividends in future periods.

factors.summary: The supply chain faces significant risks from both fuel shortages and harsh weather conditions. Fuel shortages could lead to increased expenses and service disruptions, while severe weather events can reduce demand, disrupt transportation, and impact revenue and operating expenses.

evidence.text: We may be susceptible to fuel shortages at certain locations that could cause us to incur additional expense to ensure adequate supply on a timely basis and to prevent a disruption to our service schedules.
evidence.subsection: We may be adversely impacted by fluctuations in the availability and price of diesel fuel.

evidence.text: Harsh winter weather or natural disasters, such as hurricanes, tornadoes and floods, can also adversely impact our performance by reducing demand and reducing our ability to transport freight, which could result in decreased revenue and increased operating expenses.
evidence.subsection: Our results of operations may be affected by seasonal factors, harsh weather conditions and disasters.

factors.summary: The company intends to pay a quarterly cash dividend for the foreseeable future, but future payments are not guaranteed and depend on various factors including financial condition and cash needs.

evidence.text: We intend to pay a quarterly cash dividend to holders of our common stock for the foreseeable future; however, dividend payments are subject to approval by our Board of Directors, and are restricted by applicable state law limitations on distributions to shareholders as well as certain covenants under our revolving credit facility. As a result, future dividend payments are not guaranteed and will depend upon various factors such as our overall financial condition, available liquidity, anticipated cash needs, future prospects for earnings and cash flows, as well as other factors considered relevant by our Board of Directors.
evidence.subsection: There can be no assurance of our ability to declare and pay cash dividends in future periods.

factors.summary: Management's intention to pay dividends for the foreseeable future signals cautious confidence, as they acknowledge both the intent and the potential risks.

evidence.text: We intend to pay a quarterly cash dividend to holders of our common stock for the foreseeable future; however, dividend payments are subject to approval by our Board of Directors, and are restricted by applicable state law limitations on distributions to shareholders as well as certain covenants under our revolving credit facility.
evidence.subsection: There can be no assurance of our ability to declare and pay cash dividends in future periods.

factors.summary: The company identifies financial condition, liquidity, cash needs, earnings, and cash flows as key factors that could impact future dividend payments and overall performance.

evidence.text: future dividend payments are not guaranteed and will depend upon various factors such as our overall financial condition, available liquidity, anticipated cash needs, future prospects for earnings and cash flows, as well as other factors considered relevant by our Board of Directors.
evidence.subsection: There can be no assurance of our ability to declare and pay cash dividends in future periods.
